sections.title: Current Research
paragraphs: I am a scientific employee in the Department of Machine Dynamics and Acoustics at TH Wildau. My current work investigates acoustic and vibration-based condition monitoring of rail-vehicle components, with emphasis on loose bolted joints and structural defects. Related outputs include the Forum Acusticum preprint and the DAGA SIAMIS paper.

In parallel, I conduct doctoral research at the Chair of Acoustics and Haptics at TU Dresden. My PhD work focuses on acoustic vehicle alerting systems for electric trucks. Key outputs include the JAES journal article, the electric-truck masking study, and conference papers on semantic AVAS modeling, warning-sound design, and electric-truck AVAS requirements.

sections.title: Engineering Background
paragraphs: My background bridges research and product-oriented engineering. I have worked on turboshaft and turboprop engine design at GE Aerospace, flexible electronic sensors at Kordsa, hospital-monitoring and IoT systems as a start-up co-founder, and engineering software development in the automotive and aerospace sectors.

At the Bio-Integrated Microdevices Laboratory at Koç University, I worked on flexible piezoelectric energy harvesters and ultrasonic transducer modeling. The resulting outputs include an Integrated Ferroelectrics article, a wind-energy harvester article, my master's thesis, and an IEEE IUS paper.

sections.title: Research Approach
paragraphs: I work from measurement to interpretation: define the engineering question, design a controlled experiment, acquire reproducible data, apply statistical or signal-processing models, and translate the result into a design or diagnostic decision.

This approach has led to peer-reviewed work across acoustics, vibration perception, energy harvesting, and ultrasonic transducers. My Scientific Reports article on whole-body vibration perception is one example. The complete publication list is available on the Publications & CV page and on Google Scholar.

footer.aboutText: Acoustics researcher and mechanical engineer working on psychoacoustics, vehicle sound, vibro-acoustic diagnostics, sensing, and experimental engineering.

projects.title: Selected Research & Engineering Work
projects.subtitle: Projects spanning psychoacoustics, condition monitoring, sensing, and engineering development

items.title: Electric Truck AVAS Sound Design
items.subtitle: User-centered psychoacoustic development of warning sounds
items.description: Designed and evaluated acoustic vehicle alerting systems for electric trucks using semantic profiling, psychoacoustic listening tests, binaural synthesis, and sound-quality analysis.

items.title: Psychoacoustic Modeling of Vehicle Sounds
items.subtitle: Connecting signal metrics with semantic perception
items.description: Developed consumer-centered semantic attributes and perceptual models for electric-vehicle sounds using listening-test data, psychoacoustic metrics, statistics, and multivariate analysis.

items.title: Electric-Truck Masking Thresholds
items.subtitle: Audibility limits in realistic vehicle interiors
items.description: Experimentally determined auditory masking thresholds in electric-truck cabin noise to support the design of audible yet non-intrusive warning and information sounds.

items.title: Bolted-Joint Condition Monitoring
items.subtitle: Vibro-acoustic detection of looseness and nonlinear behavior
items.description: Investigated how tightening torque and loosened joints affect acoustic and vibration responses, including single-tone and frequency-modulated excitation for rail-component diagnostics.

items.title: Flexible Piezoelectric Energy Harvesters
items.subtitle: PVDF-TrFE devices for structural-health monitoring
items.description: Designed, modeled, fabricated, and tested flexible polymer energy harvesters, combining acoustic-equivalent circuits, finite-element simulation, and micro/nanofabrication.

items.title: Medical IoT & Patented Monitoring Systems
items.subtitle: Connected devices for safer hospital workflows
items.description: Co-founded a hospital-digitalization start-up and developed sensing and communication concepts for infusion pumps, invasive devices, ventilation monitoring, and nasogastric-tube systems.

items.title: TH Wildau - Scientific Employee
items.description: Research in acoustic and vibration-based condition monitoring of rail-vehicle components, focusing on loose bolted joints and structural defects.

items.title: TU Dresden - Doctoral Researcher
items.description: Doctoral research on electric-truck AVAS sound design, psychoacoustic testing, semantic evaluation, warning-sound audibility, and binaural sound synthesis.

items.title: GE Aerospace - Design Engineer
items.description: Design engineering work on CT7/T700 turboshaft and turboprop engine programs.

items.title: TEDAVEM Information Systems - Co-Founder
items.description: Co-founded a hospital-digitalization start-up developing IoT monitoring concepts and integrated communication electronics for medical equipment.

items.title: Kordsa - Flexible Electronics Researcher
items.description: Worked in thin films and flexible electronic sensors; developed LabVIEW software for experimental testing.

items.title: Koç University - MSc Mechanical Engineering
items.description: Research in flexible polymer energy harvesters, PMUTs, micro/nanofabrication, and acoustic equivalent-circuit modeling.

items.title: Renault Group - Automotive R&D Intern
items.description: Developed a CATIA automation tool exceeding 20,000 lines of code to support manipulation of 3D parts in R&D workflows.

items.title: Koç University - BSc Mechanical Engineering
items.description: Completed undergraduate mechanical engineering studies and contributed to wearable sensor-system research for venous dislodgement detection.
